skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Guides agents through test verification, branch integration choices, pull request creation, and safe cleanup after implementation work is complete.
skill.summary: Finish completed development work with structured git integration and cleanup steps.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command examples, plus filesystem and weak cryptography patterns. The weak cryptography and filesystem alerts are false positives, but the git commands can merge, push, delete branches, and remove worktrees, so users should review actions before execution.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Modifying Git Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run external git, gh, and test commands. These commands are expected for the workflow, but they can merge branches, push to a remote, delete a branch, or remove a worktree.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown in the workflow and have clear repository side effects. The context is legitimate development branch completion, which lowers maliciousness but not operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Command Substitution in Pull Request Body
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The pull request command uses shell command substitution to build a body from a here document. The shown content is static template text, but shell expansion can be risky if a user adds untrusted text later.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is directly present. The included here document is static, so there is no current injection evidence, but the pattern can become unsafe with untrusted input.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access Alert Is Standard Error Redirection
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The filesystem alert points to redirection to /dev/null in a git merge-base command. This suppresses error output and does not read project files or exfiltrate data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line only redirects stderr to /dev/null. This is a common shell pattern and not sensitive filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Has No Cryptographic Code
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts point to the description front matter. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm usage is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain descriptive text for the skill. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm selection in the file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Branch and Worktree Cleanup Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes git branch deletion and worktree removal commands. It requires typed confirmation before discard, but these commands can permanently remove local branch and worktree state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive commands are visible and the skill documents their expected side effects. Confirmation reduces accidental use but does not remove the risk.

content.value_statement: Completed feature branches often stall because the next integration step is unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured finish workflow for tests, merge choices, pull requests, and cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Checks that the project test suite passes before offering branch completion choices.
Helps determine whether the branch split from main or master.
Presents four clear choices: local merge, pull request, keep branch, or discard work.
Guides local merge, remote push, and pull request creation commands.
Requires explicit confirmation before discarding a branch.
Defines when to remove or preserve a git worktree.
limitations: Does not choose the safest option without user input.
Does not validate every project-specific test command automatically.
Requires git and GitHub CLI access for merge and pull request workflows.
Can delete local branch or worktree state when the discard path is confirmed.

use_cases.title: Complete a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Run tests, choose an integration path, and finish a branch without leaving cleanup steps ambiguous.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Push a completed branch and create a pull request with a concise summary and test plan.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Worktrees
use_cases.description: Remove temporary worktrees only after the branch outcome is known and the user has chosen cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Finish My Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill. Verify tests, then show me the available completion options for this branch.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill. If tests pass, help me push this branch and create a pull request with a summary and test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Locally With Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill. Verify tests, identify the base branch, merge locally if I approve, rerun tests, and clean up the feature branch.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Finish Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill. Review the branch state, confirm the base branch, explain any blocking test failures, and only execute the chosen finish path after my approval.

output_examples.input: Tests pass and I want to finish this branch.
output_examples.output: Implementation complete. The assistant presents four choices: merge locally, push and create a pull request, keep the branch, or discard the work.

output_examples.input: I choose to create a pull request.
output_examples.output: The assistant pushes the branch, drafts a pull request summary, includes the test plan, and preserves the worktree.

output_examples.input: I choose to discard the work.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the branch, commits, and worktree that will be deleted, then waits for exact typed confirmation.

best_practices: Run the relevant project test command before choosing a finish option.
Confirm the base branch before merging or opening a pull request.
Read destructive git commands carefully before approving cleanup.
anti_patterns: Do not merge or open a pull request when tests are failing.
Do not discard a branch without typed confirmation.
Do not remove a worktree when the branch should be kept for later.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to run the project test suite before offering completion choices.

faq.question: Can it create a pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a GitHub CLI workflow for pushing the branch and creating a pull request.

faq.question: Can it delete my branch?
faq.answer: Yes, but only in the discard workflow after explicit typed confirmation.

faq.question: Does it support git worktrees?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes rules for when to remove or preserve a worktree.

faq.question: Is it safe for protected branches?
faq.answer: It is designed for development branches. Repository protections and human review should still control protected branches.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
